revision.text:
{{皇室祭祀}} '''歳旦祭'''（さいたんさい）は、戦前の[[祝祭日]]の中の[[皇室祭祀令]]に基づく[[祭日|小祭日]]の一つ。現在では[[新暦]][[1月1日]]（[[元日]]）に[[宮中三殿]]（[[賢所]]・皇霊殿・神殿）で行われる年始を祝う[[祭祀]]である<ref> 「年中行事事典」p324 1958年（昭和33年）5月23日初版発行 [[西角井正慶]]編 東京堂出版</ref>。
元日の[[神嘉殿]]南庭において[[天皇]]が親行する[[四方拝]]に続いて、同日早朝午前5時30分から宮中三殿において[[掌典職|掌典長]]が主宰し、[[祝詞]]をあげ、午前5時40分ごろ四方拝を済ませた[[黄櫨染御袍]]姿の天皇が拝礼し、[[黄丹袍]]姿の[[皇太子]]が続いて拝礼する。
[[神宮]]をはじめ、全国の[[神社]]においては、[[皇統]]の繁栄と、五穀豊穣と国民の加護を祈念する中祭として行われる。
== 脚注 == <references />
[[Category:1月]] [[Category:皇室の儀式]] [[Category:伊勢神宮]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さいたんさい}}

{{皇室祭祀}} '''歳旦祭'''（さいたんさい）は、戦前の[[祝祭日]]の中の[[皇室祭祀令]]に基づく[[祭日|小祭日]]の一つ。現在では[[新暦]][[1月1日]]（[[元日]]）に[[宮中三殿]]（[[賢所]]・皇霊殿・神殿）で行われる年始を祝う[[祭祀]]である<ref> 「年中行事事典」p324 1958年（昭和33年）5月23日初版発行 [[西角井正慶]]編 東京堂出版</ref>。
元日の[[神嘉殿]]南庭において[[天皇]]が親行する[[四方拝]]に続いて、同日早朝午前5時30分から宮中三殿において[[掌典職|掌典長]]が主宰し、[[祝詞]]をあげ、午前5時40分ごろ四方拝を済ませた[[黄櫨染御袍]]姿の天皇が拝礼し、[[黄丹袍]]姿の[[皇太子]]が続いて拝礼する。
[[神宮]]をはじめ、全国の[[神社]]においては、[[皇統]]の繁栄と、五穀豊穣と国民の加護を祈念する中祭として行われる。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 関連項目 == * [[皇室祭祀令]]
[[Category:1月]] [[Category:皇室の儀式]] [[Category:伊勢神宮]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さいたんさい}}

{{皇室祭祀}} '''歳旦祭'''（さいたんさい）は、戦前の[[祝祭日]]の中の[[皇室祭祀令]]に基づく[[祭日|小祭日]]の一つ。現在では[[新暦]][[1月1日]]（[[元日]]）に[[宮中三殿]]（[[賢所]]・皇霊殿・神殿）で行われる年始を祝う[[祭祀]]である<ref> 「年中行事事典」p324 1958年（昭和33年）5月23日初版発行 [[西角井正慶]]編 東京堂出版</ref>。
元日の[[神嘉殿]]南庭において[[天皇]]が親行する[[四方拝]]に続いて、同日早朝午前5時30分から宮中三殿において[[掌典職|掌典長]]が主宰し、[[祝詞]]をあげ、午前5時40分ごろ四方拝を済ませた[[黄櫨染御袍]]姿の天皇が拝礼し、[[黄丹袍]]姿の[[皇太子]]が続いて拝礼する。
[[神宮]]をはじめ、全国の[[神社]]においては、[[皇統]]の繁栄と、五穀豊穣と国民の加護を祈念する中祭として行われる。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 関連項目 == * [[皇室祭祀令]]
[[Category:1月]] [[Category:皇室の儀式]] [[Category:神道行事]] [[Category:伊勢神宮]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さいたんさい}}

{{皇室祭祀}} '''歳旦祭'''（さいたんさい）は、大日本帝国の[[祝祭日]]の中の[[皇室祭祀令]]に基づく[[祭日|小祭日]]の一つ。現在では[[新暦]][[1月1日]]（[[元日]]）に[[宮中三殿]]（[[賢所]]・皇霊殿・神殿）で行われる年始を祝う[[祭祀]]である<ref> 「年中行事事典」p324 1958年（昭和33年）5月23日初版発行 [[西角井正慶]]編 東京堂出版</ref>。
元日の[[神嘉殿]]南庭において[[天皇]]が親行する[[四方拝]]に続いて、同日早朝午前5時30分から宮中三殿において[[掌典職|掌典長]]が主宰し、[[祝詞]]をあげ、午前5時40分ごろ四方拝を済ませた[[黄櫨染御袍]]姿の天皇が拝礼し、[[黄丹袍]]姿の[[皇太子]]が続いて拝礼する。
[[神宮]]をはじめ、全国の[[神社]]においては、[[皇統]]の繁栄と、五穀豊穣と国民の加護を祈念する中祭として行われる。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 関連項目 == * [[皇室祭祀令]]
[[Category:1月]] [[Category:皇室の儀式]] [[Category:神道行事]] [[Category:伊勢神宮]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さいたんさい}}

{{皇室祭祀}} '''歳旦祭'''（さいたんさい）は、太平洋戦争前の[[祝祭日]]の中の[[皇室祭祀令]]に基づく[[祭日|小祭]]の一つ。現在では[[新暦]]1月1日（[[元日]]）に[[宮中三殿]]（[[賢所]]・皇霊殿・神殿）で行われる年始を祝う[[祭祀]]である<ref> 「年中行事事典」p324 1958年5月23日初版発行 [[西角井正慶]]編 東京堂出版</ref>。
元日の[[神嘉殿]]南庭において[[天皇]]が親行する[[四方拝]]に続いて、同日早朝午前5時30分から宮中三殿において[[掌典職|掌典長]]が主宰し、[[祝詞]]をあげ、午前5時40分ごろ四方拝を済ませた[[黄櫨染御袍]]姿の天皇が拝礼し、[[黄丹袍]]姿の[[皇太子]]が続いて拝礼する{{要出典|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 05:12 (UTC)}}。
[[神宮]]をはじめ、全国の[[神社]]においては、[[皇室の系図一覧|皇統]]の繁栄と、五穀豊穣と国民の加護を祈念する中祭として行われる<ref>神社本庁「神社祭祀規程」昭和27年2月2日 規程第11号</ref>。
==脚注== <references/>
==関連項目== *[[皇室祭祀令]]

added [[Category:Draft articles]]This draft article is taken from my draft article on complex english wikipedia. Due to technical issues I cannot easily link the source article, but it is under <nowiki>[[en:draft:XXX]]</nowiki> instead of <nowiki>[[simple:User:Immanuelle/XXX]]</nowiki> so in the future fix the interlanguagelink here [[:en:draft:{{PAGENAME}}]] to [[:en:draft:XXX]] accordingly. Do not fix the following ill [[fr:User:{{PAGENAME}}]]~~~~{{draft categories]] using [[Help:Gadget-Hot...
Created by translating the page "[[:ja:Special:Redirect/revision/94755585|歳旦祭]]"
revision.text:
{{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}}
'''The''' {{Nihongo|'''{{ill|Saiten-sai|ja|歳旦祭}}'''|歳旦祭[[ja:歳旦祭]]}} was one of [[:ja:祭日|the minor festivals]] based on [[:ja:皇室祭祀令|the Imperial Household Rites Act]] that was held on [[:ja:祝祭日|national holidays]] before the Pacific War. Today, it is [[Festival|a ritual]] to celebrate the beginning of the New Year, held at [[:ja:宮中三殿|the three Imperial Palace shrines]] ( [[:ja:賢所|the Kashikodokoro]], the Koreiden, and the Shinto shrine) on January 1st of [[:ja:新暦|the new]] calendar ( [[New Year's Day]] ) <ref> 「年中行事事典」p324 1958年5月23日初版発行 [[西角井正慶]]編 東京堂出版</ref> .
On New Year's Day, [[Emperor of Japan|the Emperor]] performs a four-way bow in the south garden [[:ja:神嘉殿|of the Shinkaden Hall]], followed by a prayer in the early morning of the same day at 5:30 a.m. in the three palace sanctuaries, presided over by [[:ja:掌典職|the Chief Cleric]], who recites [[:ja:祝詞|a prayer]] . At around 5:40 a.m., the Emperor, dressed in [[:ja:黄櫨染御袍|a yellow lacquer-dyed robe]], bows after the four-way bow, followed by [[Heir apparent|the Crown Prince]], dressed in [[:ja:黄丹袍|a yellow robe]] {{Citation needed|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 05:12 (UTC)}}</link> .
At the Ise [[Ise Grand Shrine|Jingu shrine]] and [[Shinto shrine|other shrines]] across the country, the festival is held as a medium-term festival to pray for the prosperity of [[Family tree of Japanese monarchs|the imperial line]], bountiful harvests, and protection of the people. <ref>神社本庁「神社祭祀規程」昭和27年2月2日 規程第11号</ref>
== Related item ==
* [[:ja:皇室祭祀令|Imperial Household Ceremonies Act]]
== References ==

At around 5:40 a.m., the Emperor, dressed in [[:ja:黄櫨染御袍|a yellow lacquer-dyed robe]], bows after the four-way bow, followed by [[Heir apparent|the Crown Prince]], dressed in [[:ja:黄丹袍|a yellow robe]] {{Citation needed|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 05:12 (UTC)}}</link> .
At the Ise [[Ise Grand Shrine|Jingu shrine]] and [[Shinto shrine|other shrines]] across the country, the festival is held as a medium-term festival to pray for the prosperity of [[Family tree of Japanese monarchs|the imperial line]], bountiful harvests, and protection of the people. <ref>神社本庁「神社祭祀規程」昭和27年2月2日 規程第11号</ref>

At around 5:40 a.m., the Emperor, dressed in [[:ja:黄櫨染御袍|a yellow lacquer-dyed robe]], bows after the four-way bow, followed by [[Heir apparent|the Crown Prince]], dressed in [[:ja:黄丹袍|a yellow robe]] {{Citation needed|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 05:12 (UTC)}}</link> .
At the Ise [[Ise Grand Shrine|Jingu shrine]] and [[Shinto shrine|other shrines]] across the country, the festival is held as a medium-term festival to pray for the prosperity of [[Family tree of Japanese monarchs|the imperial line]], bountiful harvests, and protection of the people. <ref>神社本庁「神社祭祀規程」昭和27年2月2日 規程第11号</ref>
== Related item ==
* [[:ja:皇室祭祀令|Imperial Household Ceremonies Act]] == Notes == {{notelist}}

At around 5:40 a.m., the Emperor, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄櫨染御袍|Kourozen-no-gohou|lt=a yellow lacquer-dyed robe|en}}, bows after the four-way bow, followed by {{ill|Heir apparent|lt=the Crown Prince|en}}, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄丹袍|a yellow robe|en}} {{Citation needed|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 05:12 (UTC)|en}}</link> .
At the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|en}} and {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=other shrines|en}} across the country, the festival is held as a medium-term festival to pray for the prosperity of {{ill|Family tree of Japanese monarchs|lt=the imperial line|en}}, bountiful harvests, and protection of the people. <ref>神社本庁「神社祭祀規程」昭和27年2月2日 規程第11号</ref>
== Related item ==

At around 5:40 a.m., the Emperor, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄櫨染御袍|Kourozen-no-gohou|lt=a yellow lacquer-dyed robe|en}}, bows after the four-way bow, followed by {{ill|Heir apparent|lt=the Crown Prince|en}}, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄丹袍|a yellow robe|en}} {{Citation needed|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 05:12 (UTC)|en}}</link> .
At the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|en}} and {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=other shrines|en}} across the country, the festival is held as a medium-term festival to pray for the prosperity of {{ill|Family tree of Japanese monarchs|lt=the imperial line|en}}, bountiful harvests, and protection of the people. <ref>神社本庁「神社祭祀規程」昭和27年2月2日 規程第11号</ref>

revision.text:
{{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated|en}}
'''The''' {{Nihongo|'''{{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|en}}'''|歳旦祭|en}} was one of {{ill|8=ja|9=祭日|the minor festivals|en}} based on {{ill|8=ja|9=皇室祭祀令|the Imperial Household Rites Act|en}} that was held on {{ill|8=ja|9=祝祭日|national holidays|en}} before the Pacific War. Today, it is {{ill|Festival|lt=a ritual|en}} to celebrate the beginning of the New Year, held at {{ill|8=ja|9=宮中三殿|Three Palace Sanctuaries|lt=the three Imperial Palace shrines|en}} ( {{ill|8=ja|9=賢所|the Kashikodokoro|en}}, the Koreiden, and the Shinto shrine) on January 1st of {{ill|8=ja|9=新暦|the new calendar|en}} ( {{ill|New Year's Day|en}} ) <ref> 「年中行事事典」p324 1958年5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Nishitsunoi Masayoshi|en}}編 東京堂出版</ref> .
On New Year's Day, {{ill|Emperor of Japan|the Emperor|en}} performs a four-way bow in the south garden {{ill|8=ja|9=神嘉殿|of the Shinkaden Hall|en}}, followed by a prayer in the early morning of the same day at 5:30 a.m. in the three palace sanctuaries, presided over by {{ill|8=ja|9=掌典職|the Chief Cleric|en}}, who recites {{ill|8=ja|9=祝詞|Norito|lt=a prayer|en}} . At around 5:40 a.m., the Emperor, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄櫨染御袍|Kourozen-no-gohou|lt=a yellow lacquer-dyed robe|en}}, bows after the four-way bow, followed by {{ill|Heir apparent|lt=the Crown Prince|en}}, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄丹袍|a yellow robe|en}} {{Citation needed|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 05:12 (UTC)|en}}</link> .
At the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|en}} and {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=other shrines|en}} across the country, the festival is held as a medium-term festival to pray for the prosperity of {{ill|Family tree of Japanese monarchs|lt=the imperial line|en}}, bountiful harvests, and protection of the people. <ref>神社本庁「神社祭祀規程」昭和27年2月2日 規程第11号</ref>
== Related item ==
* {{ill|8=ja|9=皇室祭祀令|Imperial Household Ceremonies Act|en}} == Notes == {{notelist|en}}

[[ja:歳旦祭]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated|en}}
'''The''' {{Nihongo|'''{{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|en}}'''|歳旦祭|en}} was one of {{ill|8=ja|9=祭日|the minor festivals|en}} based on {{ill|8=ja|9=皇室祭祀令|the Imperial Household Rites Act|en}} that was held on {{ill|8=ja|9=祝祭日|national holidays|en}} before the Pacific War. Today, it is {{ill|Festival|lt=a ritual|en}} to celebrate the beginning of the New Year, held at {{ill|8=ja|9=宮中三殿|Three Palace Sanctuaries|lt=the three Imperial Palace shrines|en}} ( {{ill|8=ja|9=賢所|the Kashikodokoro|en}}, the Koreiden, and the Shinto shrine) on January 1st of {{ill|8=ja|9=新暦|the new calendar|en}} ( {{ill|New Year's Day|en}} ) <ref> 「年中行事事典」p324 1958年5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Nishitsunoi Masayoshi|en}}編 東京堂出版</ref> .
On New Year's Day, {{ill|Emperor of Japan|the Emperor|en}} performs a four-way bow in the south garden {{ill|8=ja|9=神嘉殿|of the Shinkaden Hall|en}}, followed by a prayer in the early morning of the same day at 5:30 a.m. in the three palace sanctuaries, presided over by {{ill|8=ja|9=掌典職|the Chief Cleric|en}}, who recites {{ill|8=ja|9=祝詞|Norito|lt=a prayer|en}} . At around 5:40 a.m., the Emperor, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄櫨染御袍|Kourozen-no-gohou|lt=a yellow lacquer-dyed robe|en}}, bows after the four-way bow, followed by {{ill|Heir apparent|lt=the Crown Prince|en}}, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄丹袍|a yellow robe|en}} {{Citation needed|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 05:12 (UTC)|en}}</link> .
At the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|en}} and {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=other shrines|en}} across the country, the festival is held as a medium-term festival to pray for the prosperity of {{ill|Family tree of Japanese monarchs|lt=the imperial line|en}}, bountiful harvests, and protection of the people. <ref>神社本庁「神社祭祀規程」昭和27年2月2日 規程第11号</ref>
== Related item ==
* {{ill|8=ja|9=皇室祭祀令|Imperial Household Ceremonies Act|en}} == Notes == {{notelist|en}}
== References ==

[[ja:歳旦祭]]

{{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated|en}}
'''The''' {{Nihongo|'''{{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|en}}'''|歳旦祭|en}} was one of {{ill|8=ja|9=祭日|the minor festivals|en}} based on {{ill|8=ja|9=皇室祭祀令|the Imperial Household Rites Act|en}} that was held on {{ill|8=ja|9=祝祭日|national holidays|en}} before the Pacific War. Today, it is {{ill|Festival|lt=a ritual|en}} to celebrate the beginning of the New Year, held at {{ill|8=ja|9=宮中三殿|Three Palace Sanctuaries|lt=the three Imperial Palace shrines|en}} ( {{ill|8=ja|9=賢所|the Kashikodokoro|en}}, the Koreiden, and the Shinto shrine) on January 1st of {{ill|8=ja|9=新暦|the new calendar|en}} ( {{ill|New Year's Day|en}} ) <ref> 「年中行事事典」p324 1958年5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Nishitsunoi Masayoshi|en}}編 東京堂出版</ref> .
On New Year's Day, {{ill|Emperor of Japan|the Emperor|en}} performs a four-way bow in the south garden {{ill|8=ja|9=神嘉殿|of the Shinkaden Hall|en}}, followed by a prayer in the early morning of the same day at 5:30 a.m. in the three palace sanctuaries, presided over by {{ill|8=ja|9=掌典職|the Chief Cleric|en}}, who recites {{ill|8=ja|9=祝詞|Norito|lt=a prayer|en}} . At around 5:40 a.m., the Emperor, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄櫨染御袍|Kourozen-no-gohou|lt=a yellow lacquer-dyed robe|en}}, bows after the four-way bow, followed by {{ill|Heir apparent|lt=the Crown Prince|en}}, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄丹袍|a yellow robe|en}} {{Citation needed|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 05:12 (UTC)|en}}</link> .
At the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|en}} and {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=other shrines|en}} across the country, the festival is held as a medium-term festival to pray for the prosperity of {{ill|Family tree of Japanese monarchs|lt=the imperial line|en}}, bountiful harvests, and protection of the people. <ref>神社本庁「神社祭祀規程」昭和27年2月2日 規程第11号</ref>
== Related item ==

On New Year's Day, {{ill|Emperor of Japan|the Emperor|en}} performs a four-way bow in the south garden {{ill|8=ja|9=神嘉殿|of the Shinkaden Hall|en}}, followed by a prayer in the early morning of the same day at 5:30 a.m. in the three palace sanctuaries, presided over by {{ill|8=ja|9=掌典職|the Chief Cleric|en}}, who recites {{ill|8=ja|9=祝詞|Norito|lt=a prayer|en}} . At around 5:40 a.m., the Emperor, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄櫨染御袍|Kourozen-no-gohou|lt=a yellow lacquer-dyed robe|en}}, bows after the four-way bow, followed by {{ill|Heir apparent|lt=the Crown Prince|en}}, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄丹袍|a yellow robe|en}} {{Citation needed|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 05:12 (UTC)|en}}</link> .
At the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|en}} and {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=other shrines|en}} across the country, the festival is held as a medium-term festival to pray for the prosperity of {{ill|Family tree of Japanese monarchs|lt=the imperial line|en}}, bountiful harvests, and protection of the people. <ref>神社本庁「神社祭祀規程」昭和27年2月2日 規程第11号</ref>
== Related item ==

On New Year's Day, {{ill|Emperor of Japan|the Emperor|en}} performs a four-way bow in the south garden {{ill|8=ja|9=神嘉殿|of the Shinkaden Hall|en}}, followed by a prayer in the early morning of the same day at 5:30 a.m. in the three palace sanctuaries, presided over by {{ill|8=ja|9=掌典職|the Chief Cleric|en}}, who recites {{ill|8=ja|9=祝詞|Norito|lt=a prayer|en}} . At around 5:40 a.m., the Emperor, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄櫨染御袍|Kourozen-no-gohou|lt=a yellow lacquer-dyed robe|en}}, bows after the four-way bow, followed by {{ill|Heir apparent|lt=the Crown Prince|en}}, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄丹袍|a yellow robe|en}} {{Citation needed|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 05:12 (UTC)|en}}</link> .
At the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|en}} and {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=other shrines|en}} across the country, the festival is held as a medium-term festival to pray for the prosperity of {{ill|Family tree of Japanese monarchs|lt=the imperial line|en}}, bountiful harvests, and protection of the people. <ref>神社本庁「神社祭祀規程」昭和27年2月2日 規程第11号</ref>
== Related item ==

'''The''' {{Nihongo|'''{{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|en}}'''|歳旦祭|en}} was one of {{ill|8=ja|9=祭日|the minor festivals|en}} based on {{ill|8=ja|9=皇室祭祀令|the Imperial Household Rites Act|en}} that was held on {{ill|8=ja|9=祝祭日|national holidays|en}} before the Pacific War. Today, it is {{ill|Festival|lt=a ritual|en}} to celebrate the beginning of the New Year, held at {{ill|8=ja|9=宮中三殿|Three Palace Sanctuaries|lt=the three Imperial Palace shrines|en}} ( {{ill|8=ja|9=賢所|the Kashikodokoro|en}}, the Koreiden, and the Shinto shrine) on January 1st of {{ill|8=ja|9=新暦|the new calendar|en}} ( {{ill|New Year's Day|en}} ) <ref> 「年中行事事典」p324 1958年5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Nishitsunoi Masayoshi|en}}編 東京堂出版</ref> .
On New Year's Day, {{ill|Emperor of Japan|the Emperor|en}} performs a four-way bow in the south garden {{ill|8=ja|9=神嘉殿|of the Shinkaden Hall|en}}, followed by a prayer in the early morning of the same day at 5:30 a.m. in the three palace sanctuaries, presided over by {{ill|8=ja|9=掌典職|the Chief Cleric|en}}, who recites {{ill|8=ja|9=祝詞|Norito|lt=a prayer|en}} . At around 5:40 a.m., the Emperor, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄櫨染御袍|Kourozen-no-gohou|lt=a yellow lacquer-dyed robe|en}}, bows after the four-way bow, followed by {{ill|Heir apparent|lt=the Crown Prince|en}}, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄丹袍|a yellow robe|en}} {{Citation needed|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 05:12 (UTC)|en}}</link> .
At the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|en}} and {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=other shrines|en}} across the country, the festival is held as a medium-term festival to pray for the prosperity of {{ill|Family tree of Japanese monarchs|lt=the imperial line|en}}, bountiful harvests, and protection of the people. <ref>神社本庁「神社祭祀規程」昭和27年2月2日 規程第11号</ref>
== Related item ==
* {{ill|8=ja|9=皇室祭祀令|Imperial Household Ceremonies Act|en}} == Notes == {{notelist|en}}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Qq deprecated close]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated|en}}
'''The''' {{Nihongo|'''{{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|en}}'''|歳旦祭|en}} was one of {{ill|8=ja|9=祭日|the minor festivals|en}} based on {{ill|8=ja|9=皇室祭祀令|the Imperial Household Rites Act|en}} that was held on {{ill|8=ja|9=祝祭日|national holidays|en}} before the Pacific War. Today, it is {{ill|Festival|lt=a ritual|en}} to celebrate the beginning of the New Year, held at {{ill|8=ja|9=宮中三殿|Three Palace Sanctuaries|lt=the three Imperial Palace shrines|en}} ( {{ill|8=ja|9=賢所|the Kashikodokoro|en}}, the Koreiden, and the Shinto shrine) on January 1st of {{ill|8=ja|9=新暦|the new calendar|en}} ( {{ill|New Year's Day|en}} ) <ref> 「年中行事事典」p324 1958年5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Nishitsunoi Masayoshi|en}}編 東京堂出版</ref> .
On New Year's Day, {{ill|Emperor of Japan|the Emperor|en}} performs a four-way bow in the south garden {{ill|8=ja|9=神嘉殿|of the Shinkaden Hall|en}}, followed by a prayer in the early morning of the same day at 5:30 a.m. in the three palace sanctuaries, presided over by {{ill|8=ja|9=掌典職|the Chief Cleric|en}}, who recites {{ill|8=ja|9=祝詞|Norito|lt=a prayer|en}} . At around 5:40 a.m., the Emperor, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄櫨染御袍|Kourozen-no-gohou|lt=a yellow lacquer-dyed robe|en}}, bows after the four-way bow, followed by {{ill|Heir apparent|lt=the Crown Prince|en}}, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄丹袍|a yellow robe|en}} {{Citation needed|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 05:12 (UTC)|en}}</link> .
At the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|en}} and {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=other shrines|en}} across the country, the festival is held as a medium-term festival to pray for the prosperity of {{ill|Family tree of Japanese monarchs|lt=the imperial line|en}}, bountiful harvests, and protection of the people. <ref>神社本庁「神社祭祀規程」昭和27年2月2日 規程第11号</ref>
== Related item ==
* {{ill|8=ja|9=皇室祭祀令|Imperial Household Ceremonies Act|en}} == Notes == {{notelist|en}}
== References ==

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:completion tier 3]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated|en}}
'''The''' {{Nihongo|'''{{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|en}}'''|歳旦祭|en}} was one of {{ill|8=ja|9=祭日|the minor festivals|en}} based on {{ill|8=ja|9=皇室祭祀令|the Imperial Household Rites Act|en}} that was held on {{ill|8=ja|9=祝祭日|national holidays|en}} before the Pacific War. Today, it is {{ill|Festival|lt=a ritual|en}} to celebrate the beginning of the New Year, held at {{ill|8=ja|9=宮中三殿|Three Palace Sanctuaries|lt=the three Imperial Palace shrines|en}} ( {{ill|8=ja|9=賢所|the Kashiko-dokoro|en}}, the Koreiden, and the Shinto shrine) on January 1st of {{ill|8=ja|9=新暦|the new calendar|en}} ( {{ill|New Year's Day|en}} ) <ref> 「年中行事事典」p324 1958年5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Nishitsunoi Masayoshi|en}}編 東京堂出版</ref> .
On New Year's Day, {{ill|Emperor of Japan|the Emperor|en}} performs a four-way bow in the south garden {{ill|8=ja|9=神嘉殿|of the Shinkaden Hall|en}}, followed by a prayer in the early morning of the same day at 5:30 a.m. in the three palace sanctuaries, presided over by {{ill|8=ja|9=掌典職|the Chief Cleric|en}}, who recites {{ill|8=ja|9=祝詞|Norito|lt=a prayer|en}} . At around 5:40 a.m., the Emperor, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄櫨染御袍|Kourozen-no-gohou|lt=a yellow lacquer-dyed robe|en}}, bows after the four-way bow, followed by {{ill|Heir apparent|lt=the Crown Prince|en}}, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄丹袍|a yellow robe|en}} {{Citation needed|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 05:12 (UTC)|en}}</link> .
At the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|en}} and {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=other shrines|en}} across the country, the festival is held as a medium-term festival to pray for the prosperity of {{ill|Family tree of Japanese monarchs|lt=the imperial line|en}}, bountiful harvests, and protection of the people. <ref>神社本庁「神社祭祀規程」昭和27年2月2日 規程第11号</ref>
== Related item ==
* {{ill|8=ja|9=皇室祭祀令|Imperial Household Ceremonies Act|en}} == Notes == {{notelist|en}}
== References ==

At around 5:40 a.m., the Emperor, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄櫨染御袍|Kourozen-no-gohou|lt=a yellow lacquer-dyed robe|en}}, bows after the four-way bow, followed by {{ill|Heir apparent|lt=the Crown Prince|en}}, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄丹袍|a yellow robe|en}} {{Citation needed|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 05:12 (UTC)|en}}</link> .
At the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|en}} and {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=other shrines|en}} across the country, the festival is held as a medium-term festival to pray for the prosperity of {{ill|Family tree of Japanese monarchs|lt=the imperial line|en}}, bountiful harvests, and protection of the people. <ref>神社本庁「神社祭祀規程」昭和27年2月2日 規程第11号</ref>

[[ja:歳旦祭]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Saitan-sai]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Saitan-sai]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Saitan-sai]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Saitan-sai]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Saitan-sai]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Saitan-sai]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]

{{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated|en}}
'''The''' {{Nihongo|'''{{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|en}}'''|歳旦祭|en}} was one of {{ill|8=ja|9=祭日|the minor festivals|en}} based on {{ill|8=ja|9=皇室祭祀令|the Imperial Household Rites Act|en}} that was held on {{ill|8=ja|9=祝祭日|national holidays|en}} before the Pacific War. Today, it is {{ill|Festival|lt=a ritual|en}} to celebrate the beginning of the New Year, held at {{ill|8=ja|9=宮中三殿|Three Palace Sanctuaries|lt=the three Imperial Palace shrines|en}} ( {{ill|8=ja|9=賢所|the Kashiko-dokoro|en}}, the Koreiden, and the Shinto shrine) on January 1st of {{ill|8=ja|9=新暦|the new calendar|en}} ( {{ill|New Year's Day|en}} ) <ref> 「年中行事事典」p324 1958年5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Nishitsunoi Masayoshi|en}}編 東京堂出版</ref> .
On New Year's Day, {{ill|Emperor of Japan|the Emperor|en}} performs a four-way bow in the south garden {{ill|8=ja|9=神嘉殿|of the Shinkaden Hall|en}}, followed by a prayer in the early morning of the same day at 5:30 a.m. in the three palace sanctuaries, presided over by {{ill|8=ja|9=掌典職|the Chief Cleric|en}}, who recites {{ill|8=ja|9=祝詞|Norito|lt=a prayer|en}} . At around 5:40 a.m., the Emperor, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄櫨染御袍|Kourozen-no-gohou|lt=a yellow lacquer-dyed robe|en}}, bows after the four-way bow, followed by {{ill|Heir apparent|lt=the Crown Prince|en}}, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄丹袍|a yellow robe|en}} {{Citation needed|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 05:12 (UTC)|en}}</link> .
At the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|en}} and {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=other shrines|en}} across the country, the festival is held as a medium-term festival to pray for the prosperity of {{ill|Family tree of Japanese monarchs|lt=the imperial line|en}}, bountiful harvests, and protection of the people. <ref>神社本庁「神社祭祀規程」昭和27年2月2日 規程第11号</ref>

At the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|en}} and {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=other shrines|en}} across the country, the festival is held as a medium-term festival to pray for the prosperity of {{ill|Family tree of Japanese monarchs|lt=the imperial line|en}}, bountiful harvests, and protection of the people. <ref>神社本庁「神社祭祀規程」昭和27年2月2日 規程第11号</ref>

{{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated|en}}
'''The''' {{Nihongo|'''{{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|en}}'''|歳旦祭|en}} was one of {{ill|8=ja|9=祭日|the minor festivals|en}} based on {{ill|8=ja|9=皇室祭祀令|the Imperial Household Rites Act|en}} that was held on {{ill|8=ja|9=祝祭日|national holidays|en}} before the Pacific War. Today, it is {{ill|Festival|lt=a ritual|en}} to celebrate the beginning of the New Year, held at {{ill|8=ja|9=宮中三殿|Three Palace Sanctuaries|lt=the three Imperial Palace shrines|en}} ( {{ill|8=ja|9=賢所|the Kashiko-dokoro|en}}, the Koreiden, and the Shinto shrine) on January 1st of {{ill|8=ja|9=新暦|the new calendar|en}} ( {{ill|New Year's Day|en}} ) <ref> 「年中行事事典」p324 1958年5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Nishitsunoi Masayoshi|en}}編 東京堂出版</ref> .
On New Year's Day, {{ill|Emperor of Japan|the Emperor|en}} performs a four-way bow in the south garden {{ill|8=ja|9=神嘉殿|of the Shinkaden Hall|en}}, followed by a prayer in the early morning of the same day at 5:30 a.m. in the three palace sanctuaries, presided over by {{ill|8=ja|9=掌典職|the Chief Cleric|en}}, who recites {{ill|8=ja|9=祝詞|Norito|lt=a prayer|en}} . At around 5:40 a.m., the Emperor, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄櫨染御袍|Kourozen-no-gohou|lt=a yellow lacquer-dyed robe|en}}, bows after the four-way bow, followed by {{ill|Heir apparent|lt=the Crown Prince|en}}, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄丹袍|a yellow robe|en}} {{Citation needed|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 05:12 (UTC)|en}}</link> .
At the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|en}} and {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=other shrines|en}} across the country, the festival is held as a medium-term festival to pray for the prosperity of {{ill|Family tree of Japanese monarchs|lt=the imperial line|en}}, bountiful harvests, and protection of the people. <ref>神社本庁「神社祭祀規程」昭和27th year 2月2日 規程第11号</ref>

{{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated|en}}
'''The''' {{Nihongo|'''{{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|en}}'''|歳旦祭|en}} was one of {{ill|8=ja|9=祭日|the minor festivals|en}} based on {{ill|8=ja|9=皇室祭祀令|the Imperial Household Rites Act|en}} that was held on {{ill|8=ja|9=祝祭日|national holidays|en}} before the Pacific War. Today, it is {{ill|Festival|lt=a ritual|en}} to celebrate the beginning of the New Year, held at {{ill|8=ja|9=宮中三殿|Three Palace Sanctuaries|lt=the three Imperial Palace shrines|en}} ( {{ill|8=ja|9=賢所|the Kashiko-dokoro|en}}, the Koreiden, and the Shinto shrine) on January 1st of {{ill|8=ja|9=新暦|the new calendar|en}} ( {{ill|New Year's Day|en}} ) <ref> 「年中行事事典」p324 1958th year 5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Nishitsunoi Masayoshi|en}}編 東京堂出版</ref> .
On New Year's Day, {{ill|Emperor of Japan|the Emperor|en}} performs a four-way bow in the south garden {{ill|8=ja|9=神嘉殿|of the Shinkaden Hall|en}}, followed by a prayer in the early morning of the same day at 5:30 a.m. in the three palace sanctuaries, presided over by {{ill|8=ja|9=掌典職|the Chief Cleric|en}}, who recites {{ill|8=ja|9=祝詞|Norito|lt=a prayer|en}} . At around 5:40 a.m., the Emperor, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄櫨染御袍|Kourozen-no-gohou|lt=a yellow lacquer-dyed robe|en}}, bows after the four-way bow, followed by {{ill|Heir apparent|lt=the Crown Prince|en}}, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄丹袍|a yellow robe|en}} {{Citation needed|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 05:12 (UTC)|en}}</link> .
At the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|en}} and {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=other shrines|en}} across the country, the festival is held as a medium-term festival to pray for the prosperity of {{ill|Family tree of Japanese monarchs|lt=the imperial line|en}}, bountiful harvests, and protection of the people. <ref>神社本庁「神社祭祀規程」昭和27th year 2月2日 規程第11号</ref>
== Related item ==
* {{ill|8=ja|9=皇室祭祀令|Imperial Household Rites Act|en}} == Notes == {{notelist|en}}

{{draft categories| [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Saitan-sai]] [[Category:[[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Pages_from_Simple_English_Wikipedia]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Saitan-sai]] [[en:Saitan-sai]] [[ja:歳旦祭]]

{{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated|en}}
'''The''' {{Nihongo|'''{{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|en|12=draft}}'''|歳旦祭|en}} was one of {{ill|8=ja|9=祭日|the minor festivals|en|12=draft}} based on {{ill|8=ja|9=皇室祭祀令|the Imperial Household Rites Act|en|12=draft}} that was held on {{ill|8=ja|9=祝祭日|national holidays|en|12=draft}} before the Pacific War. Today, it is {{ill|Festival|lt=a ritual|en|12=draft}} to celebrate the beginning of the New Year, held at {{ill|8=ja|9=宮中三殿|Three Palace Sanctuaries|lt=the three Imperial Palace shrines|en|12=draft}} ( {{ill|8=ja|9=賢所|the Kashiko-dokoro|en|12=draft}}, the Koreiden, and the Shinto shrine) on January 1st of {{ill|8=ja|9=新暦|the new calendar|en|12=draft}} ( {{ill|New Year's Day|en}} ) <ref> 「年中行事事典」p324 1958th year 5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Nishitsunoi Masayoshi|en|12=draft}}編 東京堂出版</ref> .
On New Year's Day, {{ill|Emperor of Japan|the Emperor|en|12=draft}} performs a four-way bow in the south garden {{ill|8=ja|9=神嘉殿|of the Shinkaden Hall|en|12=draft}}, followed by a prayer in the early morning of the same day at 5:30 a.m. in the three palace sanctuaries, presided over by {{ill|8=ja|9=掌典職|the Chief Cleric|en|12=draft}}, who recites {{ill|8=ja|9=祝詞|Norito|lt=a prayer|en|12=draft}} . At around 5:40 a.m., the Emperor, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄櫨染御袍|Kourozen-no-gohou|lt=a yellow lacquer-dyed robe|en|12=draft}}, bows after the four-way bow, followed by {{ill|Heir apparent|lt=the Crown Prince|en|12=draft}}, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄丹袍|a yellow robe|en|12=draft}} {{Citation needed|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 05:12 (UTC)|en}}</link> .
At the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|en}} and {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=other shrines|en|12=draft}} across the country, the festival is held as a medium-term festival to pray for the prosperity of {{ill|Family tree of Japanese monarchs|lt=the imperial line|en|12=draft}}, bountiful harvests, and protection of the people. <ref>神社本庁「神社祭祀規程」昭和27th year 2月2日 規程第11号</ref>
== Related item ==
* {{ill|8=ja|9=皇室祭祀令|Imperial Household Rites Act|en|12=draft}} == Notes == {{notelist|en}}
== References ==

revision.comment: Bot: single‑shot reformat + ill→|12=draft + draft categories + iws + DEFAULTSORT + move HTML comments + meta‑cat check
Text replacement - "Japanese Imperial Rituals" to "Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace"
24 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:歳旦祭 up to rev 94755585
revision.text:
{{皇室祭祀}} '''歳旦祭'''（さいたんさい）は、太平洋戦争前の[[祝祭日]]の中の[[皇室祭祀令]]に基づく[[祭日|小祭]]の一つ。現在では[[新暦]]1月1日（[[元日]]）に[[宮中三殿]]（[[賢所]]・皇霊殿・神殿）で行われる年始を祝う[[祭祀]]である<ref> 「年中行事事典」p324 1958年5月23日初版発行 [[西角井正慶]]編 東京堂出版</ref>。
元日の[[神嘉殿]]南庭において[[天皇]]が親行する[[四方拝]]に続いて、同日早朝午前5時30分から宮中三殿において[[掌典職|掌典長]]が主宰し、[[祝詞]]をあげ、午前5時40分ごろ四方拝を済ませた[[黄櫨染御袍]]姿の天皇が拝礼し、[[黄丹袍]]姿の[[皇太子]]が続いて拝礼する{{要出典|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 05:12 (UTC)}}。
[[伊勢神宮|神宮]]をはじめ、全国の[[神社]]においては、[[皇室の系図一覧|皇統]]の繁栄と、五穀豊穣と国民の加護を祈念する中祭として行われる<ref>神社本庁「神社祭祀規程」昭和27年2月2日 規程第11号</ref>。
==脚注== <references/>
==関連項目== *[[皇室祭祀令]]
[[Category:正月]] [[Category:皇室の儀式]] [[Category:神道行事]] [[Category:伊勢神宮]] [[Category:新春の季語]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さいたんさい}}

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[歳旦祭]] to [[Saitan-sai]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

元日の[[神嘉殿]]南庭において[[天皇]]が親行する[[四方拝]]に続いて、同日早朝午前5時30分から宮中三殿において[[掌典職|掌典長]]が主宰し、[[祝詞]]をあげ、午前5時40分ごろ四方拝を済ませた[[黄櫨染御袍]]姿の天皇が拝礼し、[[黄丹袍]]姿の[[皇太子]]が続いて拝礼する{{要出典|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 05:12 (UTC)}}。

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:歳旦祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11546824]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace updated|en}}
'''The''' {{Nihongo|'''{{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}}'''|歳旦祭|en}} was one of {{ill|8=ja|9=祭日|Festival days|lt=Festival days|lt=The minor festivals|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} based on {{ill|8=ja|9=皇室祭祀令|Imperial Household Rites Act|lt=Imperial Household Rites Act|lt=The Imperial Household Rites Act|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} that was held on {{ill|8=ja|9=祝祭日|national holidays|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=|1=public holiday in the Empire of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588597}} before the Pacific War. Today, it is {{ill|Festival|lt=a ritual|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|qq=}} to celebrate the beginning of the New Year, held at [[:en:Three Palace Sanctuaries|the three Imperial Palace shrines]] ( {{ill|8=ja|9=賢所|Kashiko-dokoro|lt=Kashiko-dokoro|lt=The Kashiko-dokoro|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}, the Koreiden, and the Shinto shrine) on January 1st of {{ill|8=ja|9=新暦|the new calendar|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} ( {{ill|New Year's Day|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Day|qq=}} ) <ref> 「年中行事事典」p324 1958th year 5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Nishitsunoi Masayoshi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitsunoi Masayoshi|qq=}}編 東京堂出版</ref> .
On New Year's Day, {{ill|Emperor of Japan|the Emperor|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor of Japan|qq=}} performs a four-way bow in the south garden {{ill|8=ja|9=神嘉殿|of the Shinkaden Hall|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=|1=of the Shinkaden Hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105471801}}, followed by a prayer in the early morning of the same day at 5:30 a.m. in the three palace sanctuaries, presided over by {{ill|8=ja|9=掌典職|the Chief Cleric|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=|1=Chief Cleric of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498167}}, who recites [[:en:Norito|a prayer]] . At around 5:40 a.m., the Emperor, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄櫨染御袍|Kōrozen|lt=Kōrozen|lt=Kourozen-no-gohou|lt=a yellow lacquer-dyed robe|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}, bows after the four-way bow, followed by {{ill|Heir apparent|lt=the Crown Prince|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heir apparent|qq=}}, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄丹袍|Ōni robe|lt=Ōni robe|lt=A yellow robe|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} {{Citation needed|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 05:12 (UTC)|en}}</link> .
At the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=other shrines|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} across the country, the festival is held as a medium-term festival to pray for the prosperity of {{ill|Family tree of Japanese monarchs|lt=the imperial line|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Family tree of Japanese monarchs|qq=}}, bountiful harvests, and protection of the people. <ref>神社本庁「神社祭祀規程」昭和27th year 2月2日 規程第11号</ref>
== See Also ==
* {{ill|8=ja|9=皇室祭祀令|Imperial Household Rites Act|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} == Notes == {{notelist|en}}

On New Year's Day, {{ill|Emperor of Japan|the Emperor|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor of Japan|qq=}} performs a four-way bow in the south garden {{ill|8=ja|9=神嘉殿|of the Shinkaden Hall|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=|1=of the Shinkaden Hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105471801}}, followed by a prayer in the early morning of the same day at 5:30 a.m. in the three palace sanctuaries, presided over by {{ill|8=ja|9=掌典職|the Chief Cleric|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=|1=Chief Cleric of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498167}}, who recites [[:en:Norito|a prayer]] . At around 5:40 a.m., the Emperor, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄櫨染御袍|Kōrozen|lt=Kōrozen|lt=Kourozen-no-gohou|lt=a yellow lacquer-dyed robe|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}, bows after the four-way bow, followed by {{ill|Heir apparent|lt=the Crown Prince|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heir apparent|qq=}}, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄丹袍|Ōni robe|lt=Ōni robe|lt=A yellow robe|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} {{Citation needed|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 05:12 (UTC)|en}}</link> .
At the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=other shrines|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} across the country, the festival is held as a medium-term festival to pray for the prosperity of {{ill|Family tree of Japanese monarchs|lt=the imperial line|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Family tree of Japanese monarchs|qq=}}, bountiful harvests, and protection of the people. <ref>神社本庁「神社祭祀規程」昭和27th year 2月2日 規程第11号</ref>

On New Year's Day, {{ill|Emperor of Japan|the Emperor|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor of Japan|qq=}} performs a four-way bow in the south garden {{ill|8=ja|9=神嘉殿|of the Shinkaden Hall|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|1=of the Shinkaden Hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105471801}}, followed by a prayer in the early morning of the same day at 5:30 a.m. in the three palace sanctuaries, presided over by {{ill|8=ja|9=掌典職|the Chief Cleric|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|1=Chief Cleric of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498167}}, who recites [[:en:Norito|a prayer]] . At around 5:40 a.m., the Emperor, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄櫨染御袍|Kōrozen|lt=Kōrozen|lt=Kourozen-no-gohou|lt=a yellow lacquer-dyed robe|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}, bows after the four-way bow, followed by {{ill|Heir apparent|lt=the Crown Prince|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heir apparent|qq=}}, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄丹袍|Ōni robe|lt=Ōni robe|lt=A yellow robe|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} {{Citation needed|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 05:12 (UTC)|en}}</link> .
At the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=other shrines|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} across the country, the festival is held as a medium-term festival to pray for the prosperity of {{ill|Family tree of Japanese monarchs|lt=the imperial line|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Family tree of Japanese monarchs|qq=}}, bountiful harvests, and protection of the people. <ref>神社本庁「神社祭祀規程」昭和27th year 2月2日 規程第11号</ref>
== See Also ==
* {{ill|8=ja|9=皇室祭祀令|Imperial Household Rites Act|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} == Notes == {{notelist|en}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:歳旦祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11546824]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace updated|en}}
'''The''' {{Nihongo|'''{{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}}'''|歳旦祭|en}} was one of {{ill|8=ja|9=祭日|Festival days|lt=Festival days|lt=The minor festivals|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} based on {{ill|8=ja|9=皇室祭祀令|Imperial Household Rites Act|lt=Imperial Household Rites Act|lt=The Imperial Household Rites Act|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} that was held on {{ill|8=ja|9=祝祭日|national holidays|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|1=public holiday in the Empire of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588597}} before the Pacific War. Today, it is {{ill|Festival|lt=a ritual|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|qq=}} to celebrate the beginning of the New Year, held at [[:en:Three Palace Sanctuaries|the three Imperial Palace shrines]] ( {{ill|8=ja|9=賢所|Kashiko-dokoro|lt=Kashiko-dokoro|lt=The Kashiko-dokoro|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}, the Koreiden, and the Shinto shrine) on January 1st of {{ill|8=ja|9=新暦|the new calendar|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} ( {{ill|New Year's Day|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Day|qq=}} ) <ref> 「年中行事事典」p324 1958th year 5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Nishitsunoi Masayoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitsunoi Masayoshi|qq=}}編 東京堂出版</ref> .
On New Year's Day, {{ill|Emperor of Japan|the Emperor|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor of Japan|qq=}} performs a four-way bow in the south garden {{ill|8=ja|9=神嘉殿|of the Shinkaden Hall|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|1=of the Shinkaden Hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105471801}}, followed by a prayer in the early morning of the same day at 5:30 a.m. in the three palace sanctuaries, presided over by {{ill|8=ja|9=掌典職|the Chief Cleric|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|1=Chief Cleric of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498167}}, who recites [[:en:Norito|a prayer]] . At around 5:40 a.m., the Emperor, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄櫨染御袍|Kōrozen|lt=Kōrozen|lt=Kourozen-no-gohou|lt=a yellow lacquer-dyed robe|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}, bows after the four-way bow, followed by {{ill|Heir apparent|lt=the Crown Prince|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heir apparent|qq=}}, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄丹袍|Ōni robe|lt=Ōni robe|lt=A yellow robe|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} {{Citation needed|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 05:12 (UTC)|en}}</link> .
At the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=other shrines|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} across the country, the festival is held as a medium-term festival to pray for the prosperity of {{ill|Family tree of Japanese monarchs|lt=the imperial line|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Family tree of Japanese monarchs|qq=}}, bountiful harvests, and protection of the people. <ref>神社本庁「神社祭祀規程」昭和27th year 2月2日 規程第11号</ref>
== See Also ==

Today, it is {{ill|Festival|lt=a ritual|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|qq=}} to celebrate the beginning of the New Year, held at [[:en:Three Palace Sanctuaries|the three Imperial Palace shrines]] ( {{ill|8=ja|9=賢所|Kashiko-dokoro|lt=Kashiko-dokoro|lt=The Kashiko-dokoro|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}, the Koreiden, and the Shinto shrine) on January 1st of {{ill|8=ja|9=新暦|the new calendar|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} ( {{ill|New Year's Day|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Day|qq=}} ) <ref> 「年中行事事典」p324 1958th year 5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Nishitsunoi Masayoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitsunoi Masayoshi|qq=}}編 東京堂出版</ref> .
On New Year's Day, {{ill|Emperor of Japan|the Emperor|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor of Japan|qq=}} performs a four-way bow in the south garden {{ill|8=ja|9=神嘉殿|of the Shinkaden Hall|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|1=of the Shinkaden Hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105471801}}, followed by a prayer in the early morning of the same day at 5:30 a.m. in the three palace sanctuaries, presided over by {{ill|8=ja|9=掌典職|the Chief Cleric|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|1=Chief Cleric of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498167}}, who recites [[:en:Norito|a prayer]] . At around 5:40 a.m., the Emperor, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄櫨染御袍|Kōrozen|lt=Kōrozen|lt=Kourozen-no-gohou|lt=a yellow lacquer-dyed robe|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}, bows after the four-way bow, followed by {{ill|Heir apparent|lt=the Crown Prince|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heir apparent|qq=}}, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄丹袍|Ōni robe|lt=Ōni robe|lt=A yellow robe|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} {{Citation needed|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 05:12 (UTC)|en}}</link> .
At the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=other shrines|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} across the country, the festival is held as a medium-term festival to pray for the prosperity of {{ill|Family tree of Japanese monarchs|lt=the imperial line|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Family tree of Japanese monarchs|qq=}}, bountiful harvests, and protection of the people. <ref>神社本庁「神社祭祀規程」昭和27th year 2月2日 規程第11号</ref>
== See Also ==
* {{ill|8=ja|9=皇室祭祀令|Imperial Household Rites Act|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} == Notes == {{notelist|en}}

At the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=other shrines|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} across the country, the festival is held as a medium-term festival to pray for the prosperity of {{ill|Family tree of Japanese monarchs|lt=the imperial line|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Family tree of Japanese monarchs|qq=}}, bountiful harvests, and protection of the people. <ref>神社本庁「神社祭祀規程」昭和27th year 2月2日 規程第11号</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:歳旦祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:歳旦祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:歳旦祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11546824]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace updated|en}}
'''The''' {{Nihongo|'''{{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}}'''|歳旦祭|en}} was one of {{ill|8=ja|9=祭日|Festival days|lt=Festival days|lt=The minor festivals|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} based on {{ill|8=ja|9=皇室祭祀令|Imperial Household Rites Act|lt=Imperial Household Rites Act|lt=The Imperial Household Rites Act|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} that was held on {{ill|8=ja|9=祝祭日|national holidays|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|1=public holiday in the Empire of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588597}} before the Pacific War. Today, it is {{ill|Festival|lt=a ritual|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|qq=}} to celebrate the beginning of the New Year, held at [[:en:Three Palace Sanctuaries|the three Imperial Palace shrines]] ( {{ill|8=ja|9=賢所|Kashiko-dokoro|lt=Kashiko-dokoro|lt=The Kashiko-dokoro|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}, the Koreiden, and the Shinto shrine) on January 1st of {{ill|8=ja|9=新暦|the new calendar|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} ( {{ill|New Year's Day|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Day|qq=}} ) <ref> 「年中行事事典」p324 1958th year 5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Nishitsunoi Masayoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitsunoi Masayoshi|qq=}}編 東京堂出版</ref> .
On New Year's Day, {{ill|Emperor of Japan|the Emperor|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor of Japan|qq=}} performs a four-way bow in the south garden {{ill|8=ja|9=神嘉殿|of the Shinkaden Hall|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|1=of the Shinkaden Hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105471801}}, followed by a prayer in the early morning of the same day at 5:30 a.m. in the three palace sanctuaries, presided over by {{ill|8=ja|9=掌典職|the Chief Cleric|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|1=Chief Cleric of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498167}}, who recites [[:en:Norito|a prayer]] . At around 5:40 a.m., the Emperor, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄櫨染御袍|Kōrozen|lt=Kōrozen|lt=Kourozen-no-gohou|lt=a yellow lacquer-dyed robe|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}, bows after the four-way bow, followed by {{ill|Heir apparent|lt=the Crown Prince|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heir apparent|qq=}}, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄丹袍|Ōni robe|lt=Ōni robe|lt=A yellow robe|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} {{Citation needed|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 05:12 (UTC)|en}}</link> .
At the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=other shrines|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} across the country, the festival is held as a medium-term festival to pray for the prosperity of {{ill|Family tree of Japanese monarchs|lt=the imperial line|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Family tree of Japanese monarchs|qq=}}, bountiful harvests, and protection of the people. <ref>神社本庁「神社祭祀規程」昭和27th year 2月2日 規程第11号</ref>
== See Also ==
* {{ill|8=ja|9=皇室祭祀令|Imperial Household Rites Act|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} == Notes == {{notelist|en}}
== References ==

At the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=other shrines|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} across the country, the festival is held as a medium-term festival to pray for the prosperity of {{ill|Family tree of Japanese monarchs|lt=the imperial line|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Family tree of Japanese monarchs|qq=}}, bountiful harvests, and protection of the people. <ref>神社本庁「神社祭祀規程」昭和27th year 2月2日 規程第11号</ref>

* {{ill|8=ja|9=皇室祭祀令|Imperial Household Rites Act|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} == Notes == {{notelist|en}}

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:歳旦祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:歳旦祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:歳旦祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11546824]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace updated|en}}
'''The''' {{Nihongo|'''{{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}}'''|歳旦祭|en}} was one of {{ill|8=ja|9=祭日|Festival days|lt=Festival days|lt=The minor festivals|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} based on {{ill|8=ja|9=皇室祭祀令|Imperial Household Rites Act|lt=Imperial Household Rites Act|lt=The Imperial Household Rites Act|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} that was held on {{ill|8=ja|9=祝祭日|national holidays|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|1=public holiday in the Empire of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588597}} before the Pacific War. Today, it is {{ill|Festival|lt=a ritual|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|qq=}} to celebrate the beginning of the New Year, held at {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|en|Three Palace Sanctuaries|WD=Q7797685|lt=the three Imperial Palace shrines}} ( {{ill|8=ja|9=賢所|Kashiko-dokoro|lt=Kashiko-dokoro|lt=The Kashiko-dokoro|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}, the Koreiden, and the Shinto shrine) on January 1st of {{ill|8=ja|9=新暦|the new calendar|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} ( {{ill|New Year's Day|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Day|qq=}} ) <ref> 「年中行事事典」p324 1958th year 5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Nishitsunoi Masayoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitsunoi Masayoshi|qq=}}編 東京堂出版</ref> .
On New Year's Day, {{ill|Emperor of Japan|the Emperor|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor of Japan|qq=}} performs a four-way bow in the south garden {{ill|8=ja|9=神嘉殿|of the Shinkaden Hall|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|1=of the Shinkaden Hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105471801}}, followed by a prayer in the early morning of the same day at 5:30 a.m. in the three palace sanctuaries, presided over by {{ill|8=ja|9=掌典職|the Chief Cleric|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|1=Chief Cleric of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498167}}, who recites {{ill|Norito|en|Norito|WD=Q1760119|lt=a prayer}} . At around 5:40 a.m., the Emperor, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄櫨染御袍|Kōrozen|lt=Kōrozen|lt=Kourozen-no-gohou|lt=a yellow lacquer-dyed robe|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}, bows after the four-way bow, followed by {{ill|Heir apparent|lt=the Crown Prince|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heir apparent|qq=}}, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄丹袍|Ōni robe|lt=Ōni robe|lt=A yellow robe|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} {{Citation needed|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 05:12 (UTC)|en}}</link> .
At the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=other shrines|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} across the country, the festival is held as a medium-term festival to pray for the prosperity of {{ill|Family tree of Japanese monarchs|lt=the imperial line|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Family tree of Japanese monarchs|qq=}}, bountiful harvests, and protection of the people. <ref>神社本庁「神社祭祀規程」昭和27th year 2月2日 規程第11号</ref>
== See Also ==
* {{ill|8=ja|9=皇室祭祀令|Imperial Household Rites Act|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} == Notes == {{notelist|en}}
== References ==

[[ja:歳旦祭]]

On New Year's Day, {{ill|Emperor of Japan|the Emperor|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor of Japan|qq=}} performs a four-way bow in the south garden {{ill|8=ja|9=神嘉殿|of the Shinkaden Hall|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|1=of the Shinkaden Hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105471801}}, followed by a prayer in the early morning of the same day at 5:30 a.m. in the three palace sanctuaries, presided over by {{ill|8=ja|9=掌典職|the Chief Cleric|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|1=Chief Cleric of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498167}}, who recites {{ill|Norito|en|Norito|WD=Q1760119|lt=a prayer}} . At around 5:40 a.m., the Emperor, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄櫨染御袍|Kōrozen|lt=Kōrozen|lt=Kourozen-no-gohou|lt=a yellow lacquer-dyed robe|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}, bows after the four-way bow, followed by {{ill|Heir apparent|lt=the Crown Prince|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heir apparent|qq=}}, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄丹袍|Ōni robe|lt=Ōni robe|lt=A yellow robe|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} {{Citation needed|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 05:12 (UTC)|en}}</link> .
At the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=other shrines|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} across the country, the festival is held as a medium-term festival to pray for the prosperity of {{ill|Family tree of Japanese monarchs|lt=the imperial line|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Family tree of Japanese monarchs|qq=}}, bountiful harvests, and protection of the people. <ref>神社本庁「神社祭祀規程」昭和27th year 2月2日 規程第11号</ref>
== See Also ==
* {{ill|8=ja|9=皇室祭祀令|Imperial Household Rites Act|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} == Notes == {{notelist|en}}

{{User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace updated|en}}

Today, it is {{ill|Festival|lt=a ritual|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|qq=}} to celebrate the beginning of the New Year, held at {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|en|Three Palace Sanctuaries|WD=Q7797685|lt=the three Imperial Palace shrines|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries}} ( {{ill|8=ja|9=賢所|Kashiko-dokoro|lt=Kashiko-dokoro|lt=The Kashiko-dokoro|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}, the Koreiden, and the Shinto shrine) on January 1st of {{ill|8=ja|9=新暦|the new calendar|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} ( {{ill|New Year's Day|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Day|qq=}} ) <ref> 「年中行事事典」p324 1958th year 5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Nishitsunoi Masayoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitsunoi Masayoshi|qq=}}編 東京堂出版</ref> .
On New Year's Day, {{ill|Emperor of Japan|the Emperor|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor of Japan|qq=}} performs a four-way bow in the south garden {{ill|8=ja|9=神嘉殿|of the Shinkaden Hall|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|1=Shinkaden Hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105471801}}, followed by a prayer in the early morning of the same day at 5:30 a.m. in the three palace sanctuaries, presided over by {{ill|8=ja|9=掌典職|the Chief Cleric|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|1=Chief Cleric of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498167}}, who recites {{ill|Norito|en|Norito|WD=Q1760119|lt=a prayer|1=Norito}} . At around 5:40 a.m., the Emperor, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄櫨染御袍|Kōrozen|lt=Kōrozen|lt=Kourozen-no-gohou|lt=a yellow lacquer-dyed robe|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}, bows after the four-way bow, followed by {{ill|Heir apparent|lt=the Crown Prince|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heir apparent|qq=}}, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄丹袍|Ōni robe|lt=Ōni robe|lt=A yellow robe|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} {{Citation needed|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 05:12 (UTC)|en}}</link> .
At the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=other shrines|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} across the country, the festival is held as a medium-term festival to pray for the prosperity of {{ill|Family tree of Japanese monarchs|lt=the imperial line|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Family tree of Japanese monarchs|qq=}}, bountiful harvests, and protection of the people. <ref>神社本庁「神社祭祀規程」昭和27th year 2月2日 規程第11号</ref>
== See Also ==
* {{ill|8=ja|9=皇室祭祀令|Imperial Household Rites Act|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} == Notes == {{notelist|en}}

'''The''' {{Nihongo|'''{{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}}'''|歳旦祭|en}} was one of {{ill|8=ja|9=祭日|Festival days|lt=Festival days|lt=The minor festivals|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} based on {{ill|8=ja|9=皇室祭祀令|Imperial Household Rites Act|lt=Imperial Household Rites Act|lt=The Imperial Household Rites Act|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} that was held on {{ill|8=ja|9=祝祭日|national holidays|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|1=Public holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588597}} before the Pacific War. Today, it is {{ill|Festival|lt=a ritual|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|qq=}} to celebrate the beginning of the New Year, held at {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|en|Three Palace Sanctuaries|WD=Q7797685|lt=the three Imperial Palace shrines|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries}} ( {{ill|8=ja|9=賢所|Kashiko-dokoro|lt=Kashiko-dokoro|lt=The Kashiko-dokoro|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}, the Koreiden, and the Shinto shrine) on January 1st of {{ill|8=ja|9=新暦|the new calendar|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} ( {{ill|New Year's Day|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Day|qq=}} ) <ref> 「年中行事事典」p324 1958th year 5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Nishitsunoi Masayoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitsunoi Masayoshi|qq=}}編 東京堂出版</ref> .
On New Year's Day, {{ill|Emperor of Japan|the Emperor|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor of Japan|qq=}} performs a four-way bow in the south garden {{ill|8=ja|9=神嘉殿|of the Shinkaden Hall|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|1=Shinkaden Hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105471801}}, followed by a prayer in the early morning of the same day at 5:30 a.m. in the three palace sanctuaries, presided over by {{ill|8=ja|9=掌典職|the Chief Cleric|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|1=Chief Cleric of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498167}}, who recites {{ill|Norito|en|Norito|WD=Q1760119|lt=a prayer|1=Norito}} . At around 5:40 a.m., the Emperor, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄櫨染御袍|Kōrozen|lt=Kōrozen|lt=Kourozen-no-gohou|lt=a yellow lacquer-dyed robe|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}, bows after the four-way bow, followed by {{ill|Heir apparent|lt=the Crown Prince|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heir apparent|qq=}}, dressed in {{ill|8=ja|9=黄丹袍|Ōni robe|lt=Ōni robe|lt=A yellow robe|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} {{Citation needed|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 05:12 (UTC)|en}}</link> .
At the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=other shrines|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} across the country, the festival is held as a medium-term festival to pray for the prosperity of {{ill|Family tree of Japanese monarchs|lt=the imperial line|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Family tree of Japanese monarchs|qq=}}, bountiful harvests, and protection of the people. <ref>神社本庁「神社祭祀規程」昭和27th year 2月2日 規程第11号</ref>
